title: haloragis hamata
intro: Haloragis hamata, also known as poison pie, is a plant species in the haloragaceae family, native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to have a number of health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various ailments. However, it's worth noting that more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential interactions.
